---
title: "Home"
title-block-banner: false
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.profile-hero}
::: {.profile-copy}
# Li Tang

## Computational Genomics · Genome Regulation · Mitochondrial Biology

My research investigates how genetic variation and genome organization shape cellular function and human disease. I develop computational and AI-driven approaches to uncover regulatory mechanisms across the nuclear and mitochondrial genomes.

::: {.profile-links}
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=3zkQ6d0AAAAJ){.custom-button}
[Publication](publication.qmd){.custom-button .secondary-button}
[Contact](contact.qmd){.custom-button .secondary-button}
:::
:::

::: {.profile-photo-card}
::: {.photo-placeholder aria-label="Photo placeholder"}
Photo
:::
:::

::: {.education-card}
### EDUCATION/TRAINING

**Yale University**<br>
*Postdoctoral Associate (Lake Lab, Department of Genetics)*

**Central South University**<br>
*Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering*<br>
*M.S. in Computer Science and Engineering*<br>
*B.S. in Internet of Things Engineering*
:::
:::

::: {.theme-grid}
::: {.theme-card .theme-nuclear}
### Nuclear Genome Regulation and 3D Genome Architecture

I study how genome sequence, chromatin organization, and long-range regulatory interactions control gene expression and cellular identity. My work uses machine learning, deep learning, and computational genomics to model three-dimensional genome organization and investigate its roles in development and disease.

::: {.keyword-list}
- 3D genome organization
- Chromatin architecture
- Gene regulation
- Deep learning
- Functional genomics
:::
:::

::: {.theme-card .theme-mito}
### Mitochondrial Genetics and Biology

I investigate how mitochondrial DNA variation shapes mitochondrial function, evolution, and human disease. My work combines artificial intelligence, population-scale genomics, and evolutionary analysis to improve mitochondrial variant interpretation and uncover biologically meaningful patterns of mtDNA variation.

::: {.keyword-list}
- mtDNA variant interpretation
- Heteroplasmy
- Population genomics
- Mitochondrial evolution
- Human disease
:::
:::
:::
